mission.title: Install and Configure Redpanda
mission.description: Redpanda is a high-performance streaming data platform that is compatible with the Kafka API. It is designed for developers seeking a simpler, faster alternative to traditional streaming solutions, eliminating the need for ZooKeeper and JVM. Key trade-offs include its focus on performance and ease of use versus the maturity and ecosystem of Kafka.

steps.title: Add the Redpanda Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Redpanda Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add redpanda https://charts.redpanda.com

helm repo update

```

> This is the official repository for Redpanda Helm charts.
> Updates your local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install Redpanda CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions required for Redpanda to function properly in your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/redpanda-data/redpanda/v25.3.10/deploy/crds/redpanda.vectorized.io_redpandas.yaml

```

> This command installs the necessary CRDs.

steps.title: Install Redpanda using Helm
steps.description:
This command installs Redpanda in the specified namespace with the desired version.

```bash

helm install redpanda redpanda/redpanda --namespace redpanda --create-namespace --version 25.3.10

```

> This installs Redpanda in the 'redpanda' namespace.

steps.title: Create a Redpanda cluster resource
steps.description:
This step creates a Custom Resource (CR) to define your Redpanda cluster configuration.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vectorized.io/v1beta1
kind: Redpanda
metadata:
  name: my-redpanda
  namespace: redpanda
spec:
  replicas: 3
  configuration:
    auto_create_topics_enabled: true
EOF

```

> This creates a Redpanda cluster with 3 replicas.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vectorized.io/v1beta1
kind: Redpanda
metadata:
  name: my-redpanda
  namespace: redpanda
spec:
  replicas: 3
  configuration:
    auto_create_topics_enabled: true
EOF

steps.title: Verify Redpanda cluster status
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the Redpanda cluster to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get redpandas -n redpanda

```

> You should see your Redpanda cluster listed with a status of 'Ready'.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Redpanda
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Redpanda installation and associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall redpanda --namespace redpanda

kubectl delete namespace redpanda

```

> This command uninstalls the Redpanda release.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Redpanda
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data. This step will upgrade Redpanda to the specified version.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n redpanda -o name | xargs kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade redpanda redpanda/redpanda --namespace redpanda --version 25.3.10

kubectl get redpandas -n redpanda

```

> Drains the nodes to prepare for upgrade.
> This command upgrades Redpanda to the specified version.
> Verify that the Redpanda cluster is running the new version.
commands: kubectl get pods -n redpanda -o name | xargs kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to Redpanda broker'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Redpanda broker may not be running or is unreachable.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the Redpanda pods with 'kubectl get pods -n redpanda'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No such host'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The service name may not be correctly configured or the service is not running.

**Fix:**
Verify the service configuration with 'kubectl get svc -n redpanda'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated for Redpanda.

**Fix:**
Check node resource availability with 'kubectl describe nodes'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Redpanda cluster is not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** One or more pods in the Redpanda cluster are not in a running state.

**Fix:**
Use 'kubectl get pods -n redpanda' to identify any pods that are not running and troubleshoot accordingly.

versionNotes.changes: Improved performance for topic partitioning and added support for new Kafka API features.
versionNotes.deprecations: No specific deprecations noted in this version.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the Redpanda cluster listed with a status of 'Ready' when running 'kubectl get redpandas -n redpanda'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources.
